name: El Palancar - Charco Redondo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de Charco Redondo – circular por senda El Palancar
description: Hike the moderate 5.0-mile El Palancar - Charco Redondo Reservoir Loop, offering reservoir views and unique historical features.
region_intro: Hiking around Los Barrios offers diverse landscapes within the province of Cadiz, Spain. The region features varied terrain, from moderate paths with reservoir views to more challenging routes in mountainous areas. Hikers can explore natural features such as the Charco Redondo Reservoir and the Valdeinfierno Gorge. The area provides a range of trails suitable for different fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Los Barrios The most popular hiking route is El Palancar - Charco Redondo Reservoir Loop, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Charco Redondo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valdeinfierno Trail, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail leads through varied terrain, including sections near the Valdeinfierno Gorge. Local hikers also love the Montera del Torero – Valdeinfierno Gorge loop from Hohler Felsen, a 3.7 miles (5.9 km) trail leading through the Valdeinfierno Gorge, often completed in about 1 hour 41 minutes. Hiking around Los Barrios is defined by reservoir vistas, gorges, and diverse terrain, offering options for different ability levels. The network includes easy paths and more challenging routes in elevated areas. The routes in Los Barrios are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Los Barrios's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Los Barrios?
A: Los Barrios offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails. These include 8 easy routes, 12 moderate paths, and 2 more challenging options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Los Barrios?
A: Yes, Los Barrios has several easy trails perfect for families and beginners. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31539159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montera del Torero – Valdeinfierno Gorge loop from Hohler Felsen&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.7 miles (5.9 km) long and leads through the scenic Valdeinfierno Gorge.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Los Barrios?
A: Many of the trails in Los Barrios are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995982593/el-palancar-charco-redondo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Palancar - Charco Redondo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning reservoir views on a 5.0-mile (8.1 km) circuit.

Q: Are there any hikes with waterfalls in the Los Barrios area?
A: Yes, you can find trails that feature waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31536295" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoyo de Don Pedro Viewpoint – Prior Waterfall loop from El Acebuche&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.5-mile (13.7 km) route that includes the Prior Waterfall, offering a refreshing natural highlight.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes around Los Barrios?
A: Hiking in Los Barrios offers a variety of scenic views. You can expect panoramic vistas of the Charco Redondo Reservoir, the dramatic Valdeinfierno Gorge, and elevated 'Strait viewpoints' that provide expansive outlooks over the surrounding landscapes and potentially the Strait of Gibraltar.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks to see while hiking in Los Barrios?
A: Absolutely. Beyond the natural beauty, the region is rich in landmarks. You can explore nearby historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/670899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de la Frontera Castle&lt;/a&gt; or discover fascinating natural formations such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3748096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Cave&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features various highlights including other castles and caves.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Los Barrios?
A: The trails in Los Barrios are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful reservoir vistas, and the well-maintained paths through gorges and elevated areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Los Barrios?
A: Los Barrios is suitable for hiking year-round due to its mild climate. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer excursions. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes ideal, while winter provides cooler, often clear conditions.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Los Barrios dog-friendly?
A: While specific regulations can vary by trail or protected area, many natural trails in Los Barrios are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you carry water for them, especially on longer routes.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Los Barrios?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Los Barrios offers routes with significant elevation gains, some exceeding 1,000 feet. These trails often traverse mountainous terrain and may require good fitness and sure-footedness. There are 2 difficult routes available for those looking for a more strenuous adventure.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on hikes in Los Barrios?
A: The elevation gains on hikes in Los Barrios vary significantly. Easy trails might have minimal ascents, while moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995950997/valdeinfierno-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeinfierno Trail&lt;/a&gt; can feature around 575 feet (175 meters) of elevation gain. More challenging routes in the mountainous areas can involve over 1,000 feet of climbing.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Los Barrios?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and natural attractions in Los Barrios. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages or local tourism resources, but many starting points for trails are accessible by car.
tips: The Charco Redondo Reservoir is located in the heart of the Alcornocales Natural Park and can be visited by following a circular path known as El Palancar that starts right next to the A-381 motorway. A very quiet and picturesque place that is worth visiting.

faqs.question: How long does the El Palancar - Charco Redondo Reservoir Loop typically take to hike?
faqs.answer: The main circular path is approximately 8.12 km (about 5 miles) long and can take around 2 hours and 15 minutes to complete. If you include stops for photos, water, and enjoying the scenery, expect the entire trail to take approximately 2-3 hours.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally described as moderate, making it suitable for a range of fitness levels. While some sections are easily accessible, others feature varied surfaces and can be humid with rocks and roots.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the El Palancar - Charco Redondo Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near the A-381 motorway, and parking is typically available in that vicinity. Look for designated areas close to the trailhead within the municipality of Los Barrios.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Alcornocales Natural Park. While specific dog rules for this exact trail aren't detailed, generally, dogs are permitted in natural parks but often require being kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure other hikers' safety. It's always best to check the latest regulations for Parque Natural Los Alcornocales before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but consider that some sections can be very humid. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures and lush scenery, making them ideal. During summer, an early start is recommended to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for hiking the El Palancar - Charco Redondo Reservoir Loop or for entering the Alcornocales Natural Park itself. However, it's always good practice to respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, including sections that can be very humid with abundant stones and rocks covered in ferns, moss, and vines. You'll traverse through a gall oak forest, a repopulated pine forest, and areas with rich Mediterranean scrub. The return path runs parallel to the reservoir shore, winding through gall oaks and cork oaks.

faqs.question: What are the main sights and landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll enjoy impressive panoramic views of the Charco Redondo Reservoir and the Sierra de Montecoche, especially from the Mirador del Palancar. Other notable sights include traditional 'moriscos' (restored huts of former coal miners), an anthropomorphic tomb carved into rock, and a source of 'ferruginous water' (iron-rich water). Depending on water levels, you might also spot a stone resembling a half-buried dinosaur skull near the reservoir shore.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered an ideal route for families. Its moderate difficulty and varied points of interest make it an engaging experience for different age groups, though some sections might require careful footing due to varied terrain.

faqs.question: What should I know about hiking in Alcornocales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: When hiking in Alcornocales Natural Park, be aware that it's a protected natural environment. Stick to marked paths, do not disturb wildlife, and carry out all your trash. Some sections of the trail can be humid and rocky, so appropriate footwear is essential. The park is known for its diverse flora, including gall oaks and cork oaks, and offers a rich natural environment.

faqs.question: Are there any challenging or overgrown sections on the trail?
faqs.answer: While the main loop is generally well-maintained, some descriptions mention an 'old path' that is 'completely overgrown' and 'very tiring.' It's advisable to stick to the clearly marked main circular route to avoid less maintained or more challenging alternative sections.
